---
title: "Judith Hoeller"
description: "Principal Investigator"
image: ../assets/avatar-placeholder.svg
sortorder: 1
---

Judith is a theoretical and computational scientist who leverages her foundational training in mathematics and physics to understand how the brain processes complex visual information. After earning degrees at ETH Zurich and completing a PhD in theoretical condensed matter physics at Yale University, she shifted her focus to systems neuroscience as a postdoctoral associate at HHMI's Janelia Research Campus.
Outside the lab, Judith enjoys spending time outdoors, whether hiking in parks, exploring new places, or swimming in fresh lakes.

[Email](mailto:jhoeller@nd.edu) · [Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=FicZM4wAAAAJ&hl=en) · [ORCID](https://orcid.org/0009-0004-7261-4156)
